summary: First-finish smoke test of Moukis tech (FR). Backend auth + CRUD (contact, blog) all functional via API. Frontend landing, contact form validation & submit, blog list/article, admin login, tabs, and post creation all work. CRITICAL integration bug: backend serializes Mongo IDs with alias `_id` in JSON responses, but frontend AdminDashboard reads `c.id` / `p.id`. This makes admin DELETE (blog & contact) and mark-read UI calls hit `/api/blog/undefined` → 500, and all row-level data-testids become `del-post-undefined`, breaking targeted testing. Non-blocker: spec listed slug `sylla-repair` but seeded slugs are French title-based (as intended).
passed: POST /api/auth/login returns access_token + user; wrong creds → 401
GET /api/contact requires auth (401 anonymous, 200 with token)
GET /api/blog/{slug} → 200 for valid slug, 404 for unknown
POST/PUT/DELETE /api/blog with Bearer token work (content min 5 chars)
Landing page: hero-book-btn scroll + hero-call-btn tel:+33758964620
Contact form validation: empty submit → error-name/email/message shown; invalid email → error-email; on valid submit form clears + success toast
Blog list /blog renders cards; article page navigates on click
Messages tab displays submitted contact (Alice) and Blog tab lists posts; new-post-btn → editor → save-post-btn creates post visible in list

failed.issue: Backend responses use `_id` field (Pydantic alias) while frontend AdminDashboard reads `p.id`/`c.id`. Row-level ids resolve to undefined, breaking Delete Post (500 via /api/blog/undefined), Delete Contact, Mark-Read, and edit-post actions.
failed.evidence: curl GET /api/blog and /api/contact return keys starting with `_id`. UI click on delete triggers Uncaught runtime error toast 'Request failed with status code 500'. curl DELETE /api/blog/undefined → 500.

failed.issue: PUT /api/blog/{id} requires full BlogCreate body (title, content min 5). Not a partial update. Not necessarily a bug but worth noting for admin edit flow.

action_items: Fix Mongo id serialization: either (a) set FastAPI route response with `response_model_by_alias=False` (or serialize models with `model_dump(by_alias=False)`) so JSON contains `id` not `_id`, OR (b) change frontend AdminDashboard/AuthContext to read `_id` (map on load). Recommended: change backend to emit `id` for consistency with POST /api/contact which already returns `id`.
After fix, retest: admin delete post, delete contact, mark-as-read, edit post.
